data.title: KINDERCARE LRN Non-GAAP EPS of $0.23 misses by $0.04, revenue of $668.2M misses by $40.97M
data.description:
• KINDERCARE LRN press release (NYSE:KLC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.23 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $668.2M misses by $40.97M.
  • KLC maintains guidance for FY2025 consisting of revenue to be ~$2.75B to $2.85B, adjusted EBITDA to be ~$310M to $325M, and adjusted EPS to be ~$0.75 to $0.85.

data.title: eToro stock posts double-digit gains in market debut after IPO prices above range
data.description:
eToro (NASDAQ:ETOR) shares surged 31% in their first day of trading on the Nasdaq after the Israeli stock and crypto trading app raised some $310M in an initial public offering that priced above its expectations. 

Shares were briefly up over 40% earlier in the session.

The stock's opening price was $69.69, well above its IPO price of $52, giving it a market cap of $5.6B. The offering included nearly 6M newly issued shares, priced above the expected $46-$50 range, alongside a comparable number sold by existing shareholders.

At the IPO price, the social investing platform was initially valued at about $4.2B.

SA analyst is calling ETOR a speculative Buy on the view that the stock will be "highly volatile," especially during the first few sessions. He views it more as a near-term trade than a long-term investment.

data.title: Apple looking to add eye-scrolling feature to Vision Pro: report
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is working on a feature that would allow Vision Pro users to scroll using just their eyes, Bloomberg reported.
  • The new feature is being tested as part of visionOS3, a yet-to-be released version of the mixed reality headset's operating system, the news outlet added, citing people with knowledge of the matter.
  • Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • The $3,500 device, which was introduced in June 2023 and went on sale in February 2024, already allows users to look at apps and then pick them with finger pinching. This would take that capability a step further, the news outlet added, citing sources.
  • The report comes after Apple unveiled several new accessibility features on Tuesday, including the capability for humans to use brain computer interfaces to control their iPhones.
  • A new version of visionOS, along with Apple's other operating systems, are set to be unveiled at its annual developer conference, set for June 9.

data.title: Sanofi will invest at least $20B in the U.S., focus on R&D spends
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) will invest at least $20B in the U.S. through 2030 focusing on research and manufacturing, the company said on Wednesday.

This move comes at a time when major pharmaceutical companies like Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Eli Lilly (LLY) and Gilead Sciences (GILD) have also announced similar U.S. investments in recent months amid President Donald Trump's tariff threats on pharma products.

Sanofi mentioned that of the total planned investment, it will substantially increase spending in the U.S. on research and development.

"The company also plans to expand its US manufacturing capacity, both through direct investments in Sanofi sites, as well as through partnerships with other domestic manufacturers, to help ensure the production of medicines in the US," the France-based company said in a statement.

data.title: Reed's signals improved fulfillment rates and distribution gains while advancing functional beverage rollout
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Reed's, Inc. (REED) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Cyril Wallace, the newly appointed CEO, opened by highlighting operational progress and strategic changes, emphasizing the impact of a recent $10 million private placement. He stated, "We began deploying these funds in mid-January to improve our inventory position, reinvest in personnel, double down on our sales and marketing efforts." Wallace noted a significant reduction in short shipments, from approximately 20% at the start of the quarter to under 2% by April, describing this as a "fundamental shift in how we operate. For the first time in years, production is keeping pace with sales, enabling us to proactively meet demand rather than react to it."
  • The company appointed Ruud Bakker to the Board of Directors, bringing more than 25 years of beverage industry leadership experience from Red Bull, Diageo, and Heineken. Wallace stated, "We believe his expertise will enhance our ability to create value for both shareholders and customers going forward."
  • Reed's launched a new multifunctional soda line featuring organic ginger, prebiotic fiber, and adaptogenic mushrooms, with national distribution secured in Sprouts Farmers Market and active resets at major retailers including Kroger, Giant Carlisle, Hannaford, and Duane Reade. Wallace explained, "It represents a natural extension of our better-for-you portfolio and positions us at the forefront of the Baltic beverage market."
  • Notable distribution gains include expanded presence at Albertsons Safeway for Reed's Ginger Ale, a national program with Flying Cauldron at Albertsons, and new SKUs at Sprouts, Costco, Giant Carlisle, and Trader Joe's. Wallace mentioned, "These distribution gains reflect the growing demand across our core, functional and alcohol portfolios and we believe they will contribute meaningfully to our top line performance in the quarters ahead."
  • The transition from glass bottles to cans remains on track, with positive reception from both retailers and consumers.
  • Doug McCurdy, CFO, reported, "Net sales for the first quarter of 2025 increased 4.5% to $10 million compared to $9.6 million in the year ago quarter... Gross margin was 33.9% compared to 35.6% in the year-ago quarter."

Outlook

  • Wallace signaled expectations for "modest gross margin pressure due to seasonal promotion activity and higher trade ban particularly tied to the launch of the functional beverage line." He stated the company remains focused on generating strong margins in the back half of the year.
  • Management did not provide specific revenue or earnings guidance. When asked about guidance, McCurdy responded, "As a general approach in the past and going forward, we haven't provided guidance. So we'll just no comment there."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q1 2025 were $10 million, up from $9.6 million in the prior year period, driven by higher volumes with recurring national customers and favorable secondary display opportunities.
  • Gross profit for the quarter was flat at $3.4 million, while gross margin declined to 33.9% from 35.6% due to higher cost of goods sold associated with increased operating capacity and inventory.
  • Delivery and handling costs were $1.6 million, or 16.2% of net sales, compared to $1.5 million, or 15.7% of net sales. SG&A expenses rose to $3.5 million from $2.6 million, attributed to investments in personnel and marketing.
  • Operating loss for the quarter was $1.7 million, or (-$0.04) per share, versus $0.7 million, or (-$0.16) per share, in the prior year. Modified EBITDA loss was $1.6 million, compared to $0.4 million previously.
  • Cash used in operating activities increased, with $5.4 million used in Q1 2025, up from $2.4 million the previous year, driven by higher inventory purchases. Cash at quarter end was $4.9 million, with total debt at $9.6 million.

Q&A

  • Sean McGowan, ROTH Capital Partners: "Operating expenses, those were higher than we had expected... Can you tell us, what we should expect from those two operating expense lines going forward?" Doug McCurdy: "The increase in SG&A is largely attributed to upfront investments in personnel and marketing... We believe that the right set of steps to support improved cash flow performance in the second half of 2025."
  • McGowan: "When you said second quarter gross margin could be pressured do you mean down year-over-year or not as high as they might otherwise be?" McCurdy: "Our expectation on the gross margin side is that we may see some seasonal compression particularly in trade discounts or trade spend... and there's also some additional investment in the functional beverage launch as well."
  • Will Abanduha, Private Investor: "Can you guys provide any sort of kind of revenue guidance of what you're kind of targeting for the year?... do you have any time line to get the stock relisted on a major exchange?" Wallace: "No comment on the stock listing on the New York Stock Exchange." McCurdy: "As a general approach in the past and going forward, we haven't provided guidance. So we'll just no comment there."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on operating expenses and gross margin, expressing slight concern about rising costs and margin pressure, but maintained a neutral to slightly cautious tone.
  • Management remained confident and optimistic about operational improvements and future performance, but was measured and non-committal when pressed for guidance or specifics on relisting. Wallace stated, "I'm encouraged by the operational improvements being made and the strong alignment I've seen across the team."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone shifted from questions about inventory constraints and demand sustainability to a more nuanced focus on cost control, while management’s tone transitioned from defensive explanations of past constraints to proactive optimism about operational execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Current quarter featured a new CEO, Cyril Wallace, and the appointment of Ruud Bakker to the board, reflecting a leadership transition and strategic refresh.
  • Both quarters cited the $10 million capital raise and its deployment to address inventory constraints, but current quarter remarks emphasized improved fulfillment rates and a proactive supply chain.
  • The rollout of the multifunctional soda line advanced, with the current quarter highlighting national distribution and early traction, while the previous quarter discussed the initial launch and expected rollout.
  • Management’s confidence increased in discussing operational execution, shifting from concerns about demand to highlighting regained shelf space and expanded distribution.
  • Analysts shifted focus from existential questions on inventory and demand to detailed operational and cost concerns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing monitoring of global trade dynamics, particularly tariffs affecting ginger and packaging materials, and is evaluating alternative suppliers to mitigate potential disruptions.
  • Seasonal promotion activity and higher trade spend, especially for the functional beverage launch, are expected to pressure gross margins in Q2.
  • No specific timeline or commitment was given on stock relisting, despite investor inquiries.

Final Takeaway

Reed's management pointed to marked operational progress in Q1 2025, including sharply improved fulfillment rates, expanded national distribution, and the launch of an innovative functional beverage line. While gross margin faces near-term pressure due to promotional investments and input costs, the company highlighted a strengthened supply chain, enhanced inventory positions, and growing retail traction as the foundation for sustainable growth. Leadership transitions and measured investment in people and marketing signal a renewed focus on execution and long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Everus signals $3B–$3.1B 2025 revenue target while expanding backlog 41% and advancing M&A strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Everus Construction Group, Inc. (ECG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Jeff Thiede, Chief Executive Officer, opened by highlighting a strong start to 2025, noting "Our first quarter revenue increased 32%, driven by continued strength in our electrical and mechanical segment." Thiede pointed to broad-based revenue growth across end markets, especially data centers, and emphasized "continued strength in key submarkets, including our data center business." He acknowledged a modest revenue decline in the transmission and distribution (T&D) segment due to weather-related delays but stated, "Our T&D project execution was solid, enabling us to grow our T&D segment EBITDA during the quarter, even with the revenue decline."
  • Thiede stressed the company’s proactive approach to tariff and trade uncertainties, saying, "During times like these, it is critical we remain disciplined and stick to our Everus playbook," and detailed efforts such as early material procurement and contract terms to mitigate risk.
  • The CEO highlighted a substantial backlog increase: "Our total backlog at the end of the first quarter was up 10% from year-end and up 41% from the prior year period, with growth in both E&M and T&D."
  • Expanding on growth drivers, Thiede noted, "E&M backlog... increased 46% from the prior year period, driven by growth in several of our key submarkets, including data center, manufacturing, government, and hospitality." He reiterated, "We are confident we can continue this trend moving forward."
  • On strategic initiatives, Thiede discussed the acquisition of a new prefabrication facility and the addition of Tim Sznewajs as Vice President of Corporate Development and Strategy, stating, "Tim will play a critical role in our growth strategy, and he is uniquely positioned to help build out our M&A capabilities."
  • Max Marcy, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "Revenues for the first quarter of 2025 were $826.6 million, an increase of 32% compared to the same period last year." Marcy confirmed, "Our first quarter EBITDA margin was 7.5%, consistent with the prior year period." He also noted, "At March 31st, total backlog was $3.1 billion, up 10% from December 31, and up 41% from March 31 of 2024."

Outlook

  • Marcy affirmed, "We are affirming our 2025 guidance that calls for revenues in the range of $3 billion to $3.1 billion and EBITDA in the range of $210 million to $225 million." He cautioned, "It is early in the year and project timing is always difficult to predict. Additionally, the current macro environment adds another layer of uncertainty."
  • Management reiterated confidence in long-term targets, with Thiede stating, "We expect our 4EVER strategy to drive us toward a long-term financial framework of organic revenue growth in a range of 5% to 7% compound annual growth, which combined with our disciplined focus on operational excellence should drive EBITDA growth of 7% to 9% on a compound annual basis."

Financial Results

  • Marcy reported, "E&M revenue increased 47%, partially offset by a 2% decline in T&D." E&M segment revenue reached $648.2 million, and T&D revenue was $185 million.
  • E&M EBITDA was $49.5 million, up from $32.8 million in the same period last year, resulting in an EBITDA margin of 7.6%. T&D EBITDA was $20.1 million, with an EBITDA margin of 10.9%.
  • CapEx was $18.5 million in the first quarter, supporting the new prefab facility and equipment to fuel growth. The company held $54.3 million of unrestricted cash and $296.2 million of gross debt as of March 31.

Q&A

  • Brent Thelman, D.A. Davidson: Asked about the impact of large, long-lead projects on revenue timing. Thiede responded, "Some of these projects have a longer front end... sometimes that extends the duration of the project on the front end."
  • Thelman: Inquired about T&D workload outlook amid seasonal delays. Thiede replied, "The T&D segment is a very important part of our business, and we look to build upon our success with our customers... we're very well positioned with the expertise of our field and management teams."
  • Ian Zaffino, Oppenheimer: Queried about the outlook for non-backlog business and hiring. Thiede emphasized, "The non-backlog work is very, very important to us, especially the MSA work." Marcy added, "We feel really good about deploying those resources to our backlog and how that translates to results for the rest of the year in our guidance."
  • Zaffino: Asked about hospitality backlog and weather delays. Thiede confirmed an increase in Las Vegas backlog and, regarding T&D, stated, "We see this as a great opportunity to be able to have more projects in the T&D segment for Everus in the remainder of the year and beyond."
  • Quinten Helmer, Stifel: Asked about pharmaceutical manufacturing opportunities. Thiede said, "It is one of the markets that we are focusing on to be able to deploy resources and position ourselves to grow."
  • Chris Senyek, Wolfe Research: Asked about quarterly cadence and tariff impacts. Thiede acknowledged some revenue pull forward to Q1 and macroeconomic uncertainties. On tariffs, Thiede said, "We're very proactive when it comes to procuring long-lead equipment and materials... we believe we've mitigated that risk for our customers."
  • Senyek: Asked about corporate cost run rate. Marcy responded, "That'll probably go up a little bit for the remainder of the year... the increase overall between our insurance... you'll see the $28 million in total. No change to the overall number."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive to neutral tone, seeking clarity on revenue timing, backlog conversion, and exposure to macro risks such as tariffs and weather delays.
  • Management remained confident and constructive, frequently reiterating strategic discipline, proactive risk management, and strong demand trends. Thiede consistently expressed optimism: "We are confident we have the right strategy in place and the right team to execute on our plan."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was similarly upbeat but more focused on execution amid early-year uncertainties, while analysts' questions reflected heightened interest in the effects of large projects and risk mitigation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 revenue and EBITDA was reaffirmed at $3 billion to $3.1 billion and $210 million to $225 million, unchanged from the previous quarter.
  • Backlog growth accelerated in Q1 2025, up 10% sequentially and 41% year-over-year, compared to a 38% annual increase reported in Q4 2024.
  • E&M growth strengthened, with a 47% increase in revenue in Q1 2025 versus 21% in Q4 2024, while T&D saw a modest decline after prior growth.
  • The quarter saw a new strategic hire (Vice President of Corporate Development and Strategy) and a major investment in a Kansas City prefab facility, signaling greater emphasis on M&A and organic expansion.
  • Management’s confidence in execution remained strong, with emphasis shifting to risk management in tariffs and material procurement compared to the prior quarter’s focus on broader market optimism.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged "tariff and trade uncertainties are creating very dynamic operating conditions" and described mitigation through early procurement and contract terms.
  • Weather-related delays impacted T&D segment revenue but were partially offset by strong project execution and backlog growth.
  • Macro environment uncertainty and project timing were noted as risks to the annual outlook.
  • Analysts questioned the cadence of revenue recognition and the potential for margin pressures from tariffs and corporate cost increases.

Final Takeaway

Everus Construction Group started 2025 with robust revenue and backlog growth, supported by expanding demand in key submarkets such as data centers, hospitality, and high-tech manufacturing. Management reaffirmed both its full-year revenue and EBITDA outlook while emphasizing disciplined execution, proactive risk mitigation around tariffs, and a strengthened focus on M&A and capital deployment. The company’s leadership cited a strong pipeline and track record as foundations for continued growth, with confidence in navigating macroeconomic and project timing uncertainties through its established strategy and experienced team.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Intel falls as investors digest CFO comments on limited foundry commitments
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares were down 4% during Wednesday trading as investors weighed recent comments from Intel Chief Financial Officer David Zinsner regarding external customer commitments for its foundry.

"While it's felt challenging to get caught up in progress, we've actually made a lot of headway," Zinsner said during the J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference in Boston on Tuesday, regarding its foundry business. "Unfortunately, to get to a point of where 18A is ramping in the fab is a painful process that requires a lot of capital investment."

He said chip manufacturing is similar to tackling and blocking. 

"We talk about a pipeline model, and we get some customers," he added. "Then we test chips and some customers fall out, and there's a certain amount of customers that hang in there. So, committed volume is not significant right now, for sure. But, I think we've got to partly prove ourselves a little bit with our own product and eat our own dog food here. And then it's likely we start to see more engagement around customers. I hear more customers wanting a second source."

Intel remains on track for the foundry business to break even sometime in 2027. 

Competitors such as Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) were up 5.8% and 0.7%, respectively, by early afternoon trading.

"The one thing about 18A is that it was originally developed for internal use by Intel," Zinsner said. "It was not fully developed as a foundry node, such as 14A ... The first 18A customer is going to be Intel products, it's Panther Lake. The first SKU is expected to be out by the end of the year. We always expected the majority of the volume with 18A was going to be internal. We don't need a lot of external customers to drive a reasonable return on 18A."

Operating a foundry for internal products and for external customers creates unique challenges.

"The challenge of being a foundry player in our construct is that the products business competes against many of the customers that could be customers of the foundry," Zinsner said. "They have to feel comfortable that IP is getting protected and supply is getting protected."

"It doesn't require a ton to break even on foundry, it's somewhere in the single mid-digit billions of revenue that foundry has to get from external sources ... But what's important is that we drive most of our revenue through Intel products," he added. 

Zinsner also discussed some of the efforts at changing the culture at Intel by new CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

"I think it's a fair assessment that Lip-Bu isn't making massive changes," he said. "I think when he looks at the business, what he feels is the biggest issue at this point is a lack of execution. He is attacking that in a number of different ways. He is really flattening the organization, and has already done that at the executive level."

"He wants the lowest levels of the organization close to him," Zinsner said. "He's also spending a lot of time with customers ... The one area where he is spending a lot of time where it could lead to a different strategy is AI."

Tan has rolled back remote positions, forcing employees to return to the office. He also moved the engineers closer to the customers, so they can develop products that better meet clients' specific needs.

data.title: Bayer plunges as Roundup ingredient glyphosate reportedly singled out in RFK's upcoming report
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) -9.8% in Wednesday's trading as U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s upcoming "Make America Healthy Again" report is expected to single out glyphosate, the active ingredient in the company's widely-used Roundup weedkiller, The Wall Street Journal reported.

RFK's report will label pesticides as a potential cause of U.S. health problems, which has attracted pushback from some White House and agency officials who are concerned the move would disrupt the food supply chain, according to the report.

Kennedy sued over pesticides in his past career as an environmental litigator, helping win an initial $289M verdict in 2018 against Monsanto, then the maker of Roundup, for a groundskeeper who developed cancer and said he was not warned properly about the product's risks.

A branch of the World Health Organization said in 2015 that glyphosate was "probably carcinogenic to humans," but Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) has said consistently that the product is safe and has been tested extensively, and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency has said the weedkiller does not cause cancer.

data.title: Alibaba Q4 Preview: AI, cloud, U.S.-China trade deal fuel optimism
data.description:
Analysts expect Alibaba’s (NYSE:BABA) revenue growth to accelerate during its fourth-quarter earnings report, scheduled for Wednesday, May 14, before the market opens. Their optimism is driven not only by the company’s core e-commerce business but also by its AI and cloud computing segments.

Wall Street is eyeing a 27% year-on-year jump in EPS to $1.78 while revenue is expected to rise by 8.43% compared to last year to $33.32B.

BABA has surged more than 57% year-to-date, fueled by investor enthusiasm surrounding AI advancements and the recently finalized U.S.-China trade deal.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dair Sansyzbayev also believes that the trade deal and a bullish outlook on Alibaba's cloud and AI business could serve as significant positive catalysts for the Chinese e-commerce giant’s Q4 results.

The analyst noted that the company’s cloud and AI segment has quickly emerged as the primary driver of revenue growth and he expects to hear more optimistic commentary about its performance during the upcoming earnings call.

Another analyst A.J. Button, echoed similar sentiments saying that “Alibaba Cloud is currently #1 in Chinese cloud services. Its main virtue compared to the U.S. cloud giants is cheaper pricing. Within China, it is also price-competitive.

“In addition, Alibaba is generally ahead of the competition in Chinese AI models. Broadly speaking, Alibaba appears to be ahead of everyone in China except maybe DeepSeek. Since DeepSeek does not compete with Alibaba Cloud, and even provides a use case for BABA's cloud (running DeepSeek on Alibaba servers), Alibaba's position in Chinese AI appears very strong,” he added.

Commenting on the U.S.-China trade deal, Sansyzbayev described it as a pivotal development for both countries, citing the substantial volume of bilateral trade.

“Lower tariffs will not disrupt the trade between both global behemoths, which is an apparent positive factor for an e-commerce giant like BABA,” he said.

Over the last 2 years, BABA has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and surpassed revenue estimates 75% of the time. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 10 upward revisions and 3 downward moves, whereas revenue estimates have seen 9 upward revisions and 7 downward moves.
